experimentPointer.name: EX01585 - Fatty acids, 13-HODE and 15-HETE
experimentPointer.description: The goal of this experiment to determine the effects of diet with enriched with corn oil (linoleic acid constitutes 55% of fatty acid content of corn oil) affects colonic lipid content especially arachidonic acid, linoleic acid, ALOX15 metabolites of arachidonic acid and linoleic acid (13-HODE and 15-HETE), EPA and palmitic acid. Mice were fed either a diet of 20% corn oil or control diet of 5% corn oil. This pilot experiment will assess the ability to measure these lipid analytes. We are using colonic samples from mice that are of wildtype and without activation of modified genes. The mass of the tissues is an estimate. This custom one panel of 4 free fatty acids and 2 oxylipins (13-HODE and 15-HETE) was discussed with Dr. Kachman, who agreed to performing this limited targeted lipid analyses as a combined custom assay.

experimentPointer.name: EX01387 - BATCH02-NEG-V2
experimentPointer.description: MoTrPAC Human Post Suspension Tranche 1 adipose powder samples from shipment H20000902J

experimentPointer.name: EX01387 - BATCH04
experimentPointer.description: MoTrPAC Human Post Suspension Tranche 1 adipose powder samples from shipment H20000902J

experimentPointer.name: EX01355 - BATCH01
experimentPointer.description: MoTrPAC Human Tranche1 Muscle samples from shipment H20000820Y

experimentPointer.name: EX01496 - merge test 11
experimentPointer.description: MoTrPAC Human Post Suspension Tranche 3 EDTA plasma samples from shipment X20001463K

experimentPointer.name: EX01541 - merge test 8
experimentPointer.description: The aim of this project is to identify which gut metabolites are affected by periodontitis-induced alterations in the gut microbiota and how these changes contribute to colorectal cancer tumor growth. To this end, fecal microbiota from periodontitis model mice were transplanted into B6 mice, followed by inoculation with MC38 colorectal cancer cells. Plasma was subsequently collected from the heart.

experimentPointer.name: EX01541 - merge test 7
experimentPointer.description: The aim of this project is to identify which gut metabolites are affected by periodontitis-induced alterations in the gut microbiota and how these changes contribute to colorectal cancer tumor growth. To this end, fecal microbiota from periodontitis model mice were transplanted into B6 mice, followed by inoculation with MC38 colorectal cancer cells. Plasma was subsequently collected from the heart.

experimentPointer.description: The aim of this project is to identify which gut metabolites are affected by periodontitis-induced alterations in the gut microbiota and how these changes contribute to colorectal cancer tumor growth. To this end, fecal microbiota from periodontitis model mice were transplanted into B6 mice, followed by inoculation with MC38 colorectal cancer cells. Plasma was subsequently collected from the heart.

experimentPointer.description: The aim of this project is to identify which gut metabolites are affected by periodontitis-induced alterations in the gut microbiota and how these changes contribute to colorectal cancer tumor growth. To this end, fecal microbiota from periodontitis model mice were transplanted into B6 mice, followed by inoculation with MC38 colorectal cancer cells. Plasma was subsequently collected from the heart.

experimentPointer.description: The aim of this project is to identify which gut metabolites are affected by periodontitis-induced alterations in the gut microbiota and how these changes contribute to colorectal cancer tumor growth. To this end, fecal microbiota from periodontitis model mice were transplanted into B6 mice, followed by inoculation with MC38 colorectal cancer cells. Plasma was subsequently collected from the heart.

experimentPointer.name: EX01526 - BATCH16
experimentPointer.description: MoTrPAC Human Post Suspension Tranche 1 EDTA plasma samples from shipment H20001805E.

experimentPointer.name: EX01562 - TEST1 empty
experimentPointer.description: We have used lentivirus to incorporate a transgene encoding a putative toxin into HeLa lines. This experiment hopes to use the dox to turn on the gene to understand the putative in vivo substrate for the enzymatic activity of the toxin.

experimentPointer.name: EX01541-merge-test
experimentPointer.description: The aim of this project is to identify which gut metabolites are affected by periodontitis-induced alterations in the gut microbiota and how these changes contribute to colorectal cancer tumor growth. To this end, fecal microbiota from periodontitis model mice were transplanted into B6 mice, followed by inoculation with MC38 colorectal cancer cells. Plasma was subsequently collected from the heart.

experimentPointer.name: EX01562 - TEST2
experimentPointer.description: We have used lentivirus to incorporate a transgene encoding a putative toxin into HeLa lines. This experiment hopes to use the dox to turn on the gene to understand the putative in vivo substrate for the enzymatic activity of the toxin.
